items._note: EAST landing wall NORTH segment (z 6..16.0833): separates the family room (west) from the walk-in closet (east). Solid. Mirrors the west landing wall's north segment.

EAST landing wall SOUTH segment (z -11.9167..-1): separates the landing/hall from the primary bedroom. Solid. The z -1..6 gap between the segments is the OPEN MOUTH of the east central hall - mirror of the west alcove mouth (the old single primary-entry door is replaced by this open mouth).

Walk-in divider along z=6 (x -12..3.9167). Its WEST 5 ft (x -1.0833..3.9167) is the NORTH wall of the east central hall; the door here is the hall->walk-in entry (mirrors the west alcove's NW-bedroom door: 36" leaf, hinge W, opens N). West of x -1.0833 the divider is solid, so the walk-in is reached ONLY from the hall, not through the bedroom.
EAST CENTRAL HALL south wall (z=-1, x -1.0833..3.9167): 5 ft wall with a 36" door into the PRIMARY BEDROOM (mirrors the west alcove's SW-bedroom door). Hinge W, opens S into the bedroom.
EAST CENTRAL HALL back (east) wall (x=-1.0833, z -1..6): solid. Mirrors the J&J bath east wall that closes the west alcove. The primary bedroom wraps east and south of the hall.
Primary <-> en-suite (extension) separator at x=-12; door from the primary bedroom into the en-suite.
West landing wall NORTH segment (z 6..16.0833): family-room + NW-bedroom east wall. Solid.

West landing wall SOUTH segment (z -11.9167..-1): SW-bedroom east wall (backs the stair). Solid. The z -1..5 gap between the segments is OPEN — the old central hall is now merged into the E-W hall/landing.
NW bedroom / alcove divider (z=6). The 5 ft alcove wall (x 15.0833..20.0833): a 36" door centered with 12" of wall each side. Opens N into the NW bedroom.
SW bedroom / alcove divider (z=-1). The 5 ft alcove wall: a 36" door centered with 12" of wall each side. Opens S into the SW bedroom.

WEST BATH east wall (x=20.0833, z -1..6): the SINGLE bathroom entry, off the alcove (west end of the E-W hall). 2'8" door centered on the alcove (z2.5), opens W into the bath, hinged on the NORTH jamb so the leaf swings toward the open north side, clear of the toilet.
WEST BATH north wall (z=6, x 20.0833..31): SOLID. The NW bedroom no longer opens into the bath - the bath is entered only from the alcove.
WEST BATH south wall (z=-1, x 20.0833..31): SOLID. The SW bedroom no longer opens into the bath.

FAMILY ROOM south wall = north wall of the E-W hall (z=6, aligned with the NW-bedroom south wall to make one continuous line across the floor). A double-wide (6 ft) CASED OPENING - no door slab - so the family room reads as an open bay off the landing.

PRIMARY BEDROOM king (east wing): headboard CENTERED on the NORTH wall segment (x -12..-1.0833, the walk-in divider west of the hall bite; no window). Was tucked in the NE corner - now centred. Foot points S to the window wall (no door). Clears the en-suite, hall, and walk-in doors.
items.head: N

items._note: SW BEDROOM queen (room x 15.0833..31, z -11.9167..-1): headboard on the SOUTH wall, placed directly EAST of the south window (window ~x24..26.5) so it sits beside the window, not under it. Foot points N. Moved off the east wall so it no longer fouls the alcove door swing.
items.head: S

items._note: NW BEDROOM queen (room x 15.0833..31, z 6..16.0833): headboard on the NORTH wall, CENTERED between the two north windows (~x17.9 and ~x26.3) so it rests on solid wall between them, not under a window. Foot points S. Moved off the east wall so it no longer fouls the alcove door swing.
items.head: N

items._note: WEST BATH (x 20.0833..31, z -1..6). Shower on the NORTH wall (x 24..27.5), shallow 2.5 ft depth since the room is only ~7 ft N-S. Toilet in the west bay BETWEEN the west wall (x31) and the shower's west edge (x27.5), facing S with ~21 in clearance each side. Vanity in the SE corner (faces N). All clear of the entry-door swing.

PRIMARY EN-SUITE (extension x -22.92..-12, z -11.92..4). NORTH wall = full-width walk-in shower (glass opens S) with a CENTRAL opening (no door) flanked by half-height pony walls (glass above); behind each pony wall a bench runs along the side wall with a recessed product niche above it. Middle: DOUBLE-SINK vanity on the EAST wall - a single centered window (z-3.75) flanked by a mirror over each sink, wall sconces outboard. SOUTH end = an ENCLOSED WC room behind a partition at z-7.5; inswing door toward the EAST (hinge east jamb, opens S); toilet against the WC room's WEST wall.
WC room NORTH wall = partition at z=-7.5 (x -22.92..-12). 2'8" INSWING door toward the EAST end (hinge on the east jamb, opens S into the WC room).
FAMILY ROOM (landing north bay, x 3.9167..15.0833, z 5..16.0833): sofa against the west wall facing the TV on the east wall, with a rug between.
77-inch TV on the east wall facing W toward the sofa (screenFt = 5.6 ft wide, 16:9 -> ~3.15 ft tall -> 6.42 ft / 77 in diagonal), on a 6 ft media console.
